entries.title: 1930 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Uruguay. Uruguay defeated Argentina to win the title. The first World Cup, held with 13 teams, all invited rather than qualified.

entries.title: 1934 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Italy. Italy defeated Czechoslovakia to win the title. The first World Cup with a qualifying tournament and a knockout format throughout.

entries.title: 1938 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by France. Italy defeated Hungary to win the title. Italy became the first team to retain the title.

entries.title: 1950 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Brazil. Uruguay defeated Brazil to win the title. The first World Cup after a 12-year, war-interrupted gap; Uruguay stunned hosts Brazil in the 'Maracanazo'.

entries.title: 1954 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Switzerland. West Germany defeated Hungary to win the title. West Germany's win, the 'Miracle of Bern', over a heavily favored Hungary.

entries.title: 1958 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Sweden. Brazil defeated Sweden to win the title. A 17-year-old Pelé announced himself to the world.

entries.title: 1962 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Chile. Brazil defeated Czechoslovakia to win the title. Brazil retained the title despite losing Pelé to injury early in the tournament.

entries.title: 1966 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by England. England defeated West Germany to win the title. Hosts England won their only title, decided by a contested extra-time goal in the final.

entries.title: 1970 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Mexico. Brazil defeated Italy to win the title. Widely regarded as one of the greatest tournaments, won by a Pelé-led Brazil side.

entries.title: 1974 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by West Germany. West Germany defeated Netherlands to win the title. West Germany beat the Netherlands' influential 'Total Football' side on home soil.

entries.title: 1978 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Argentina. Argentina defeated Netherlands to win the title. Hosts Argentina won their first title amid the backdrop of a military dictatorship.

entries.title: 1982 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Spain. Italy defeated West Germany to win the title. Expanded to 24 teams for the first time.

entries.title: 1986 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Mexico. Argentina defeated West Germany to win the title. Diego Maradona's 'Hand of God' and 'Goal of the Century' both came in this tournament.

entries.title: 1990 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Italy. West Germany defeated Argentina to win the title. West Germany's third title, months before German reunification.

entries.title: 1994 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by United States. Brazil defeated Italy to win the title. The final was decided on penalties for the first time in World Cup history.

entries.title: 1998 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by France. France defeated Brazil to win the title. Hosts France won their first title, expanded to 32 teams for the first time.

entries.title: 2002 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by South Korea / Japan. Brazil defeated Germany to win the title. The first World Cup held in Asia and the first with co-hosts.

entries.title: 2006 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Germany. Italy defeated France to win the title. Remembered for Zinedine Zidane's headbutt in his final match as a professional.

entries.title: 2010 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by South Africa. Spain defeated Netherlands to win the title. The first World Cup held in Africa; Spain won their first title.

entries.title: 2014 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Brazil. Germany defeated Argentina to win the title. Hosts Brazil were eliminated 7-1 by Germany in a stunning semi-final.

entries.title: 2018 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Russia. France defeated Croatia to win the title. France's second title, led by a breakout tournament from 19-year-old Kylian Mbappé.

entries.title: 2022 FIFA World Cup
entries.description: Hosted by Qatar. Argentina defeated France to win the title. The first World Cup held in the Middle East and in the northern-hemisphere winter; Argentina won on penalties.
